summary: Crisp soundboard recording of a blues‑rock party night with an epic second set – standout jams on “He's Gone”, “The Wheel” and guest‑filled highlights.
review: The show is hailed as one of the best of the ’90s, especially the second set where Steve Miller and James Cotton turn Soldier Field into a blues‑rock celebration. Jerry’s guitar work is clean and inventive, Vince shines on keys, Phil drops constant bombs, and the drummers create a 30‑minute “train space” that fans adore. The soundboard source is generally crisp, with only minor monitor‑mix quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive fan comments (average 3.8★) and strong praise for both performance and audio quality push the rating into the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix; monitor mix noted as slightly better but a bit chopped. No major dropouts or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid and rock‑focused; the second set explodes into a guest‑heavy blues jam marathon, highlighted by a 30‑minute space sequence (Saint → He's Gone → The Wheel → So Many Roads).

band_member_comments.Jerry: Clean, energized playing with intricate fingerpicking and extended solos that many reviewers called “crystal clear”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Consistently “dropping bombs,” driving the jams forward and adding power to the long space sections.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as shining without reverb; rhythm guitar work praised, especially on blues numbers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince Welnick in top form – vocally strong and keyboard work called “on point” throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (and Mickey) generate massive train‑space energy; drummers receive special kudos for the 30‑minute jam.
